sanskrit:
एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः।
आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम्॥
transliteration:
etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥ
ācaraty ātmanaḥ śreyas tato yāti parāṃ gatim
literal_meaning: Having been freed by these from the three gates of darkness, a person acts for the highest good of the self; from that he attains the supreme goal.
contextual_meaning: When lust, anger, and greed no longer hijack perception, behavior naturally aligns with intrinsic values, initiating a self-reinforcing cycle toward psychological wholeness and transpersonal purpose.
psychological_state: Post-impulsive integration: the nervous system has down-regulated threat responses, allowing prefrontal values to guide behavior; identity shifts from reactive to self-authored.
trigger_scenario: After deleting the hook-up app and walking away from a toxic workplace argument, you notice your mind is quiet enough to finish the creative project you abandoned years ago.
anchor_text: Drop lust, anger, greed; act from clarity and rise to your highest self.
